pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents portfolio management operations for ESG Credits API, including creation and management of investor portfolios containing multiple green asset positions. Enables portfolio-level impact consolidation and tracking of investments across different green assets and issuers.

codeExamples.description: Example portfolio structure showing investor portfolios with multiple green asset positions
Adds a specific investment position to an investor's portfolio for consolidated impact tracking

useCases: Create portfolios to group investor positions in multiple green assets
Track portfolio-level consolidated ESG impacts across investments
Manage investor holdings in green bonds and sustainability-linked assets
Generate portfolio reports with aggregated environmental benefits
Add or remove positions from portfolios as investments change

implementationSteps.title: Create Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio operation with investor_identifier and portfolio_identifier to establish a new portfolio for an investor

implementationSteps.title: Add Positions
implementationSteps.description: Use addPositionToPortfolio with portfolio_identifier and position_id to associate investments with the portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Portfolio Data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios or getPortfolioPositions to view all portfolios and their associated green asset positions

implementationSteps.title: Manage Portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use removePositionFromPortfolio or removePortfolio to clean up positions or entire portfolios when needed

pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Covers position management for ESG Credits API, including creation, retrieval, and removal of investor positions in green assets. Enables tracking of investment amounts, currencies, terms, and start dates with automatic impact calculation integration for ESG benefit reporting.

codeExamples.description: Example position data showing investment details including amount, currency, term, and associated asset/issuer information
Creates or updates an investment position with amount, currency, term, and optional automatic impact calculation

useCases: Register investor positions in green bonds and sustainable assets
Track investment amounts and terms for ESG impact calculations
Manage multi-currency positions across different green assets
Update position details as investments are modified
Remove positions and associated impact data when investments close

implementationSteps.title: Create Position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition with investor_identifier, asset_identifier, position_amount, currency_code, and optional reporting_year to register investments

implementationSteps.title: Specify Investment Terms
implementationSteps.description: Include position_days (default 365) and position_start_date for accurate impact calculations and certificate generation

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Positions
implementationSteps.description: Use getPositions with investor_identifier and optional asset_identifier or issuer_identifier filters to view holdings

implementationSteps.title: Remove Position
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition with investor_identifier and position_id to delete investment records and associated impact data

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents green project management for ESG Credits API, including project registration, impact characteristic tracking, and consolidation into green frameworks. Enables management of individual renewable energy and sustainability projects with category indicators and year-specific details for impact reporting.

codeExamples.code:
[
  {
    "project_id": 1,
    "project_identifier": "NWP123",
    "project_name": "Norrbotten wind park",
    "project_allocated_amount": 11540605381,
    "project_currency_code": "SEK",
    "framework_identifier": "project-greenbond",
    "framework_name": "Project Green Bond Framework",
    "project_visibility": "private"
  },
  {
    "project_id": 2,
    "project_identifier": "SSF123",
    "project_name": "Skåne solar farm",
    "project_allocated_amount": 1634937960,
    "project_currency_code": "SEK",
    "framework_identifier": "project-greenbond",
    "framework_name": "Project Green Bond Framework",
    "project_visibility": "private"
  }
]
codeExamples.description: Example project list showing wind and solar projects with allocated amounts and framework associations

useCases: Register renewable energy projects like wind parks and solar farms
Track allocated funding amounts for individual green projects
Associate projects with green bond frameworks for consolidated reporting
Add project-level impact indicators and ESG benefit values
Build frameworks by consolidating multiple project impacts

implementationSteps.title: Create Project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject with project_identifier, name, currency_code, framework_identifier, and visibility to register green projects

implementationSteps.title: Add Year Specifics
implementationSteps.description: Use addYearSpecificsToProject to set allocated_amount and reporting_year for annual project tracking

implementationSteps.title: Configure Impact Indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject to add sub_category_id, indicator_id, and ESG benefit values for project impacts

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate into Framework
implementationSteps.description: Use buildFrameworkFromProjects with framework_identifier to consolidate multiple project impacts into a unified green framework

paragraphs: This section has operations to register projects and their impact characteristics, and one operation to consolidate projects into a green framework
Use this operation to retrieve green projects registered in ESG.Credits data repository.
check Operation parameters
Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts.

Use this operation to set up the different impact categories, indicators, and units, with specified values.

Amount of funding / use of proceeds

Use this operation to consolidate project impact data into a green framework, as an option to register the framework as a whole. The framework needs to be created and year specifics added. Projects need to refer to the framework.
buildFrameworkFromProjects

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy structure operations for ESG Credits API, including management of categories, sub-categories, indicators, units, UN Sustainable Development Goals, and EU Environmental Objectives. Enables creation of custom ESG impact taxonomies with multi-language support for green finance reporting frameworks.

codeExamples.description: Example UN SDG reference data showing goal numbers and descriptions for ESG alignment

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies with categories like renewable energy and circular economy
Define impact indicators such as CO2 emission reduction and energy production
Map green projects to UN Sustainable Development Goals
Align frameworks with EU Environmental Objectives
Create multi-language taxonomy translations for international reporting

implementationSteps.title: Create Taxonomy Structure
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory, addSubCategory, addSuperIndicator, and addIndicator operations to build hierarchical taxonomy framework

implementationSteps.title: Define Units and SDGs
implementationSteps.description: Use addUnit to specify measurement units (CO2e kg, MWh) and addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory to map UN SDGs and EU Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Taxonomy
implementationSteps.description: Use getCategories, getSubCategories, getIndicators, getUnits, getSDGs, and getEUObjectives to access complete taxonomy structure

pages.title: Translations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Translations API for ESG Credits, enabling multilingual support for taxonomy elements including categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Supports 12 languages including English, Swedish, Danish, Norwegian, Finnish, German, Polish, French, Dutch, Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian.

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all available language codes for translation support
Adds a Swedish translation for a category in the taxonomy
Adds a language translation for an indicator in the taxonomy

useCases: Create multilingual ESG taxonomy for international reporting
Add translations for environmental impact categories across European markets
Localize sustainability indicators for regional compliance frameworks
Support multilingual investor reporting in green asset portfolios

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Available Languages
implementationSteps.description: Call getLanguages operation to list all supported language codes (en, sv, dk, nb, fi, de, pl, fr, nl, es, pt, it)

implementationSteps.title: Add Category Translations
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryTranslation, addSubCategoryTranslation, addSuperIndicatorTranslation operations with category_id, reporting_year, language_code, and translation text

implementationSteps.title: Add Indicator and Unit Translations
implementationSteps.description: Complete taxonomy localization by adding translations for indicators and units using addIndicatorTranslation and addUnitTranslation operations

Translations are removed when taxonomy items are removed. hashtag getLanguages hashtag Returns all registered Languages POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to list available languages for when you are to register translations for categories, sub-categories, super-indicators, indicators, and units, when building up your taxonomy. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description "language_code" : "en" , "language" : "English" } , { "id" : 2 , "language_code" : "sv" , "language" : "Svenska" } , { "id" : 3 , "language_code" : "dk" , "language" : "Dansk" } , { "id" : 4 , "language_code" : "nb" , "language" : "Norska" } , { "id" : 5 , "language_code" : "fi" , "language" : "Suomalainen" } , { "id" : 6 , "language_code" : "de" , "language" : "Deutsch" } , { "id" : 7 , "language_code" : "pl" , "language" : "Polskie" } , { "id" : 8 , "language_code" : "fr" , "language" : "Français" } , { "id" : 9 , "language_code" : "nl" , "language" : "Nederlands" } , { "id" : 10 , "language_code" : "es" , "language" : "Español" } , { "id" : 11 , "language_code" : "pt" , "language" : "Portoguêse" } , { "id" : 12 , "language_code" : "it" , "language" : "Italiano" } ] hashtag pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Users API for managing end users, groups, and investor access permissions in ESG Credits. Provides operations to create, retrieve, and remove users, manage group memberships, and control user access to specific investor information.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new user or updates existing user information with encryption support
Adds a user to a specific group to control access to functionality
Grants a user access to specific investor information

useCases: Manage user accounts for financial institution ESG reporting platforms
Control banker and investor group access to green asset portfolios
Grant portfolio managers access to specific investor ESG data
Implement role-based permissions for ESG data administrators and end users
Restrict user access to specific investor portfolios and impact reports

implementationSteps.title: Create Users
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, and role (user or admin) to create new users with optional encryption

implementationSteps.title: Assign Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call getGroups to retrieve available groups (bankers, investors, capitalists, issuers, agents, exchanges, regulators), then use addGroupToUser to assign permissions

implementationSteps.title: Grant Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser to connect users with specific investors, controlling which portfolio and impact data they can access

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the ESG Credits API solution designed for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels. Supports banks, investment advisors, institutional investors, and venture capitalists in calculating and reporting environmental benefits from green asset investments with blockchain verification.
useCases: Banks creating investor reports for green asset portfolios in digital channels
Investment advisors providing ESG impact reporting to retail customers
Institutional investors tracking environmental benefits of green investments
Venture capitalists providing consolidated impact reports to sponsors
Issuers publishing quality-assured impact characteristics for promotional purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing verified green frameworks to issuers

implementationSteps.title: Define Taxonomy and Framework
implementationSteps.description: Build impact categories, indicators, and units using ICMA framework or custom taxonomy, supporting two levels of categories and indicators with any impact unit

implementationSteps.title: Configure Impact Calculations
implementationSteps.description: Set up green framework impact profiles as basis for calculations, consolidating benefits from green projects and tying to UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Store and Issue Certificates
implementationSteps.description: Immutably store ESG impact profiles on blockchain, issue blockchain certificates for customer investments, and prevent double-counting of green project benefits

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right Solution Overview ESG Credits API chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

pages.title: Calendar | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Calendar API for FAN Game Cube, managing scheduled games, matches, and tournament instances linked to specific Fields. Provides operations to create, list, and delete time-bound events like UEFA Champions League finals or LaLiga seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new calendar for a season or tournament (e.g., LaLiga 2025-26)
Lists all calendars or retrieves a specific calendar by ID
Response when successfully creating a new calendar, returns calendarId for linking

useCases: Create season calendars for sports leagues like LaLiga or UEFA Champions League
Schedule single-event tournaments such as championship finals
Manage multiple match calendars for weekend sports events
Link gameplay timeline to specific tournament schedules

implementationSteps.title: Create Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use new_calendar operation with a name parameter (e.g., '2025-26' or 'UEFA Champions League Final') to create a time-bound event

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Calendar ID
implementationSteps.description: Get the calendarId from the response to use when linking matches and gameplay to the calendar

implementationSteps.title: Manage Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_calendars to view all calendars or specific ones, and delete_calendar to remove obsolete schedules

pages.title: Cubes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Cubes API for FAN Game Cube, managing zones or tiles within a Field that represent unique NFTs owned by fans. Cubes are automatically generated when creating fields and support ownership tracking, pricing updates, and reward processing during live matches.

codeExamples.description: Lists cubes for a specific field with pagination support, returns cube coordinates and status
Updates pricing for multiple cubes using i_j index format, price in USD cents
Response showing cube details including coordinates, dimensions, and pagination key

useCases: List all NFT zones available for purchase on a sports field
Update cube pricing based on field location (e.g., goal areas cost more)
Track fan ownership of specific field zones as NFTs
Process rewards for cube owners when events occur in their zones
Paginate through hundreds of cubes on large fields

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Use list_cubes with fieldId and limit parameters to get cubes, handle pagination with lastEvaluatedKey for large fields

implementationSteps.title: Update Cube Pricing
implementationSteps.description: Call update_cube-price with fieldId, cubesList array (using i_j format), and priceInCents to set NFT prices

implementationSteps.title: Track Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Monitor cube status and ownership data to enable rewards and track fan engagement during live matches

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. triangle-exclamation Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. hashtag list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. hashtag list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); hashtag list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). hashtag update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) hashtag update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. pages.title: Event Types | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Event Types API for defining in-game occurrences like goals, passes, or assists in FAN Game Cube. Events are registered with spatial coordinates and timestamps, affecting cube owners whose zones match the action location with assigned point values.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new event type (e.g., 'pass') with assigned points (100) for a specific field
Updates the point value for an existing event type from 100 to 1000
Response when successfully creating an event type, returns eventTypeId for use in live event registration

useCases: Define goal events worth 1000 points for cube owners in goal zones
Create pass events worth 100 points for midfield cube owners
Set up assist events with specific point rewards
Configure sport-specific events like fouls, corners, or penalty kicks
Update event point values based on game importance or rarity

implementationSteps.title: Create Event Types
implementationSteps.description: Use new_event-type with fieldId, eventTypeName (e.g., 'pass', 'goal'), and eventTypePoints to define scoreable events

implementationSteps.title: List and Review Events
implementationSteps.description: Call list_event-types with fieldId to retrieve all configured event types and their current point values

implementationSteps.title: Update or Remove Events
implementationSteps.description: Use update_event-type to change point values or delete_event-type to remove obsolete event definitions

pages.title: Field | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Field API for creating virtual sports fields tokenized into interactive zones (CUBES). Fields represent foundational canvases for gameplay linked to real-world stadiums with customizable dimensions and cube sizes, automatically generating NFT zones upon creation.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new field representing Camp Nou stadium with dimensions in centimeters and 3x3 meter cubes
Lists all fields or retrieves a specific field by fieldId with status and configuration details
Response when successfully creating a field, returns fieldId for linking cubes and matches

useCases: Create virtual representation of Real Madrid Stadium for fan engagement
Tokenize Madison Square Garden into NFT zones for basketball games
Build Camp Nou field with customizable cube sizes for soccer matches
Generate hundreds of NFT cubes automatically when creating new stadium fields
Link real-world sports venues to digital fan experiences

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify name (e.g., 'Camp Nou'), sport type ('football'), dimensions in centimeters (fieldLength: 12000, fieldWidth: 8000), and cubeLength (300 for 3x3m zones)

implementationSteps.title: Create Field
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation which automatically generates all cubes for the field based on dimensions and cube size

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Field ID
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned fieldId to link calendars, matches, and gameplay events to this virtual stadium

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Field Represents a virtual sports field tokenized into interactive zones or sections ("CUBES"). It is the foundational canvas for all game interactions. Each field is typically linked to a real-world sports match. Examples: Real Madrid Stadium Madison Square Garden hashtag new_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Field name (e.g. Camp Nou) sport string Sport (e.g. Football) fieldLength number Field length in centimeters (e.g. 12000) fieldWidth number Field width in centimeters (e.g. 9000) cubeLength number The square size of a single cube (e.g. 300 for a cube of 3x3 meters) hashtag new_field API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag new_field Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the fieldId , which will be used to link NFT Game Cube related objects. All the cubes for that field are created when creating a new field. hashtag list_fields POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_fields parameters JSON (optional) check method parameters hashtag list_fields Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id hashtag list_fields API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js hashtag list_fields Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the list of fields you created or a single field if fieldId parameter is included in the call. hashtag delete_field POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_field parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id to delete. hashtag delete_field API Call Example delete_field API call using Node.js hashtag delete_field Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. circle-info All the cubes created and assigned to the deleted field are also deleted. pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Live Events API for recording in-match events at specific coordinates during scheduled matches. Serves as the primary mechanism to capture game data (goals, moves, fouls) and map them to cubes for scoring, supporting manual input, AI-driven detection, or custom application integration.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event (e.g., goal, pass) at coordinates x:45, y:40 during a match for cube scoring

useCases: Record goals in real-time to award points to cube owners in goal zones
Track passes and fouls with AI-driven machine learning models
Manually register chess moves through organizer dashboard
Automatically detect and push game events using BWS ML models
Integrate custom application to capture sport-specific events with timestamps

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the type of event being registered

implementationSteps.title: Register Live Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with event parameters to record the in-match occurrence at specific field coordinates

implementationSteps.title: Process Rewards
implementationSteps.description: System automatically maps event to corresponding cubes and processes scoring and rewards for cube owners based on event type points

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Live Events LiveEvent is the base API object used to record an in-match event that occurs at a specific coordinate ( x , y ) on a Field during a scheduled Match ( Calendar ). It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. hashtag new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter hashtag new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. hashtag new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. hashtag new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js hashtag new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for Match operations in the FAN Game Cube system. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific fields where events are tracked and fan interactions occur during sports games.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match with team information, start time, and match image uploaded to IPFS
Updates specific match details like description without changing other parameters
Lists matches for a calendar with match details including IPFS image URLs

useCases: Create football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team flags and details
Schedule chess tournaments between players like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Track NBA games between teams like Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears
Update match status from scheduled to playing or finished
Delete canceled matches from the calendar

implementationSteps.title: Create Match
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match operation with calendarId, startTimeInMillis, match name, description, team names and flags (base64 encoded images)

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match with matchId and calendarId to modify match properties like status (scheduled, playing, finished, canceled) or pricing

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve Match Information
implementationSteps.description: Call list_matches with calendarId to get all matches or include matchId to get specific match details with IPFS image URLs

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura circle-info Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. hashtag new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag new_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). hashtag new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js hashtag new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. hashtag update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag update_match Method Parameters circle-info Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) hashtag update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js hashtag update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. hashtag list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters hashtag list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. hashtag list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js hashtag list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. hashtag delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters hashtag delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. hashtag delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js hashtag delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing Field-Calendar relationships (Plays) in the NFT Game Cube solution. Explains how to create temporal lifecycle connections between static field configurations and dynamic calendar entries representing tournaments or seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a field-calendar relationship with description and image for a season
Lists all calendars linked to a specific field with IPFS image URLs
Response showing field-calendar relationship with IPFS-stored image URL

useCases: Link PSG Champions League 2026 calendar to stadium field configuration
Create New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season calendar for basketball court
Track multiple tournament seasons on the same sports ground
Organize field usage across different calendar periods
Delete obsolete field-calendar relationships for past seasons

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field-calendar with fieldId, calendarId, description (e.g. PSG 2025-26), and optional base64 encoded image

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Use list_field-calendars with fieldId to retrieve all calendar relationships for that field, including IPFS image URLs

implementationSteps.title: Remove Field-Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Call delete_field-calendar with both fieldId and calendarId to remove the temporal relationship

A Field defines a sports ground's static, spatial configuration (like a 12×8 football pitch), while Calendar entries dynamically represent individual matches , tournaments , or rounds on that Field. Examples: PSG Champions League 2026 New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season circle-info Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for Prize management in FAN Game Cube. Details how to create, update, list, and delete rewards (NFTs, tokens, physical goods, experiences) that users can receive based on CUBE performance in specific matches or seasons.

codeExamples.description: Creates a first-place prize for a match with name, description, and preview image
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match with IPFS image URLs
Updates prize details like HTML description without changing other properties

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top cube owners in football matches
Offer VIP match tickets as prizes for highest-scoring participants
Grant zoom calls or AMA sessions with athletes as exclusive rewards
Distribute bonus BWS tokens to winners based on cube performance
Provide physical goods or digital NFTs as season-long prizes

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Call new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1, 2, or 3), name, description, and snapshot image (base64 encoded)

implementationSteps.title: Update Prize Details
implementationSteps.description: Use update_match-prize with prizeId to modify prize properties like htmlDescription, images, or text descriptions

implementationSteps.title: List and Delete Prizes
implementationSteps.description: Call list_match-prizes to view all prizes for a match, or delete_match-prize with prizeId to remove a prize

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the FAN Game Cube platform that revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own NFT-based digital assets linked to specific field sections. Explains cube allocation, pricing strategies, revenue potential, and real-time engagement mechanics across multiple sports.
useCases: Divide football stadiums into 500-2000 cubes for fans to purchase and track match events
Tokenize 64 chess squares for tournament engagement and real-time tracking
Create basketball court cubes covering three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone
Enable cricket fans to own batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections
Allow rugby enthusiasts to purchase try zones, scrums, and midfield sections as NFTs
Generate $250,000 revenue by selling 500 cubes at $500 each for VIP events
Trade cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces with royalties for clubs
Engage fans with passive earnings through resale royalties and long-term value

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation
implementationSteps.description: Determine how many cubes to sell based on sport characteristics (500-2000 for football, 64 for chess, custom for basketball/cricket/rugby)

implementationSteps.title: Set Pricing Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Assign premium prices to strategic field areas (penalty spots, center court) and lower prices ($10+) to secondary zones for broad participation

implementationSteps.title: Integrate Purchase Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed the one-click JavaScript widget on club websites for frictionless cube purchases using standard credit cards

implementationSteps.title: Launch Fan Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Enable fans to manage cubes via mobile app with real-time match tracking, live leaderboards, and prize competitions

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here arrow-up-right . hashtag Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. hashtag NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. hashtag Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. hashtag Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. hashtag Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. hashtag How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. hashtag Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. hashtag Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube chevron-left Next FAN Game Cube API chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Cube Allocation for Different Sports NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading Pricing and Monetization Strategy Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget How Fans Can Participate and Win Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics Why Choose FAN Game Cube? pages.summary: Complete command reference page for all 73 X Bot commands organized by functional category. Provides navigation to detailed command documentation for filtering, configuration, raids, reports, leaderboards, scheduling, and administration features.
useCases: Browse all 73 commands organized by 12 functional categories
Set up first-time bot configuration with account tracking and project naming
Perform daily operations like generating reports and starting engagement raids
Create advanced named filters for complex tracking scenarios
Automate report generation with scheduled intervals
Customize engagement scoring points and leaderboard display

implementationSteps.title: First Time Setup
implementationSteps.description: Initialize with /start, add accounts with /add_accounts, configure project name with /set_project_name, generate first report with /recreate

implementationSteps.title: Daily Operations
implementationSteps.description: View latest analytics with /report, start engagement raids with /raidx, check raid progress with /raid_status

implementationSteps.title: Advanced Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Create named filters with /set_x_filtering, automate reports with /set_schedule, customize scoring with /set_points

Initialize the bot for your group /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Add X accounts to track /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Configure project name /recreate arrow-up-right - Generate your first analytics report hashtag Daily Operations /report arrow-up-right - View latest performance analytics /raidx arrow-up-right - Start a new engagement raid /raid_status arrow-up-right - Check active raid progress hashtag Advanced Configuration /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Create named filters for complex tracking /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Automate report generation /set_points arrow-up-right - Customize engagement scoring hashtag All Commands by Category hashtag Filter Setup (20 commands) Manage tracking filters for accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignore lists. Commands: /list_filters arrow-up-right - Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each. /show_filter arrow-up-right - Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags,. /add_accounts arrow-up-right - Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. /add_keywords arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. /add_cashtags arrow-up-right - Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. /add_mentions arrow-up-right - Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. /add_excludes arrow-up-right - Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. /add_ignore arrow-up-right - Adds X accounts to ignore. /remove_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes specific X accounts from tracking. /remove_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. /remove_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes specific cashtags from tracking. /remove_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes X handles from mention tracking. /remove_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes keywords from the exclusion list. /remove_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes X accounts from the ignore list. /clear_accounts arrow-up-right - Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. /clear_keywords arrow-up-right - Removes ALL keywords from the filter. /clear_cashtags arrow-up-right - Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. /clear_mentions arrow-up-right - Removes ALL mentions from the filter. /clear_excludes arrow-up-right - Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. /clear_ignore arrow-up-right - Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. hashtag Basic Commands (4 commands) Essential bot operations including help, initialization, and status checks. Commands: /help arrow-up-right - Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. /start arrow-up-right - Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. /status arrow-up-right - Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. /get_chatid arrow-up-right - Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. hashtag Advanced Filtering (3 commands) Create named filters for complex tracking scenarios and multi-project management. Commands: /set_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. /get_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. /delete_x_filtering arrow-up-right - Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. hashtag Project Configuration (10 commands) Customize project metadata including name, description, logo, and URLs for reports. Commands: /set_project_name arrow-up-right - Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the. /get_project_name arrow-up-right - Displays the current project name configured for your community. /set_project_description arrow-up-right - Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. /get_project_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current project description configured for your community. /set_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. /get_project_long_description arrow-up-right - Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. /set_project_logo arrow-up-right - Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. /get_project_logo arrow-up-right - Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. /set_project_urls arrow-up-right - Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. /get_project_urls arrow-up-right - Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. hashtag Raid Commands (11 commands) Coordinate X (Twitter) engagement raids to amplify post visibility and reach. Commands: /raidx arrow-up-right - Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. /raid_status arrow-up-right - Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. /raid_stop arrow-up-right - Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. /raid_history arrow-up-right - Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. /set_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior,. /get_raid_defaults arrow-up-right - Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration,. /set_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. /get_raid_message_behavior arrow-up-right - Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. /set_raid_image arrow-up-right - Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. /auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. /get_auto_raid_config arrow-up-right - Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after. hashtag Schedule Management (3 commands) Configure automated report generation schedules with custom intervals. Commands: /set_schedule arrow-up-right - Sets a schedule for when reports should be automatically generated. /get_schedule arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scheduled time for automatic report generation. /delete_schedule arrow-up-right - Removes the configured daily schedule for automatic report generation. hashtag Calendar & Time (2 commands) Manage calendar settings and time periods for report generation cycles. Commands: /set_calendar arrow-up-right - Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. /get_calendar arrow-up-right - Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often. hashtag Reports (2 commands) Generate and manage performance analytics reports for tracked X content. Commands: /report arrow-up-right - Displays the most recently generated leaderboard report for your community. /recreate arrow-up-right - Triggers immediate generation of a fresh report by fetching latest X (Twitter) data and creating new leaderboards. hashtag Leaderboard Customization (10 commands) Customize scoring points, colors, titles, and display settings for leaderboard reports. Commands: /set_points arrow-up-right - Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_points arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics. /set_colors arrow-up-right - Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views). /get_colors arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics. /set_top_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. /set_best_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. /set_engagement_title arrow-up-right - Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. /get_titles arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including. /set_top_count arrow-up-right - Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. /get_top_count arrow-up-right - Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report. hashtag Topic Management (2 commands) Configure Telegram topic/thread settings for bot operation. Commands: /set_topic arrow-up-right - Configures which forum topic (thread) the bot should send reports to in Telegram groups that use forum-style topics. /get_topic arrow-up-right - Displays which forum topic (thread) is currently configured to receive bot reports. hashtag Admin (1 commands) Administrative functions for managing bot notifications and permissions. Commands: /add_admin arrow-up-right - Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as. hashtag Subscription & Credits (5 commands) Manage billing, subscriptions, credits, and usage tracking. Commands: /subscription arrow-up-right - Provides access to Stripe customer portal for subscription management including billing history,. /buy arrow-up-right - Initiates the purchase process for X Bot Professional subscription. /get_credits arrow-up-right - Displays the current credit usage and availability for the bot. /payment_success arrow-up-right - Handles successful payment confirmation after Stripe checkout completion. /payment_cancelled arrow-up-right - Handles payment cancellation when users exit Stripe checkout without completing payment. pages.title: Admin | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation for the /add_admin command which designates specific group administrators to receive bot notifications via private messages. Helps reduce notification noise by limiting alerts about credit exhaustion, subscription renewals, or errors to designated admins only.

codeExamples.description: Designates three specific users to receive bot notifications as administrators

useCases: Designate specific admins to receive credit exhaustion warnings
Limit subscription renewal notifications to bot managers only
Reduce notification noise for admins who don't manage the bot
Configure error alert recipients for technical administrators

By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators. paragraphs: Designates specific group administrators to receive important bot notifications via private messages, such as credit exhaustion warnings, subscription renewals, or error alerts. By default, the bot sends notifications to all group admins, but you can use this command to limit notifications to specific admins who manage the bot. This helps reduce notification noise for admins who don't need bot alerts. All designated users must already be group administrators.

pages.title: Advanced Filtering | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Advanced filtering commands documentation covering /set_x_filtering, /get_x_filtering, and /delete_x_filtering. Enables creation of complex named filters using X API query syntax with logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators for sophisticated tracking scenarios.

codeExamples.description: Creates a named filter tracking mentions of a specific project or hashtag
Creates a filter tracking posts from specific accounts containing certain keywords
Creates a filter for trending hashtags while excluding specific terms and accounts

useCases: Track mentions of project accounts or branded hashtags across X
Monitor posts from key opinion leaders (KOLs) containing specific keywords
Filter trending hashtags while excluding spam or scam content
Create complex queries combining multiple criteria with Boolean logic
Review and delete sophisticated filtering setups
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Advanced Filtering hashtag /set_x_filtering Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands. Admin Only Copy /set_x_filtering project_mentions mention:@MyProject OR #MyProjectToken Copy /set_x_filtering my_kols from:@CryptoHayes OR from:@IncomeSharks keywords:BWS Copy /set_x_filtering trending #DeFi OR #crypto OR #blockchain exclude:scam ignore:@SpamBot Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter query_syntax required value hashtag /get_x_filtering Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering. Admin Only Copy /get_x_filtering hashtag /delete_x_filtering Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter_name required my_filter Previous Basic Commands chevron-left Next Project Configuration chevron-right Last updated 10 days ago Was this helpful? /set_x_filtering /get_x_filtering /delete_x_filtering Was this helpful? sun-bright desktop moon Copy /delete_x_filtering my_kols sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: Creates advanced named filters with complex X API query syntax. Supports combining multiple criteria using logical operators (OR, AND) and special operators (from:, keywords:, mention:, exclude:, ignore:). Use this for sophisticated tracking scenarios requiring multiple conditions and Boolean logic that go beyond simple add/remove commands.

Lists all advanced named filters configured for your group. Shows each filter's name and its complete X API query syntax, helping you understand what content is being tracked. Use this to review your sophisticated filtering setup created with /set_x_filtering.

Deletes advanced named filters created with /set_x_filtering. Remove a specific filter by name to stop tracking that particular query, or use the wildcard "*" to delete all advanced filters at once. This does not affect simple filters managed with add/remove commands.

pages.title: Basic Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Basic commands documentation covering /help, /start, /status, and /get_chatid. Provides essential bot operations for initialization, help information, operational status checks, and retrieving Telegram group chat IDs for technical configurations.

codeExamples.description: Displays complete list of available commands with descriptions based on user role
Initializes the bot in groups or shows welcome message with Add to Group button in private chats
Checks if bot is operational and provides documentation link
Displays the Telegram group's unique chat ID for technical integrations

useCases: Initialize bot in Telegram group after installation
Get complete command reference differentiated by admin vs regular user permissions
Verify bot is responsive after installation or during troubleshooting
Retrieve unique chat ID for technical configurations and API integrations
Handle payment confirmations after subscription purchases
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Basic Commands hashtag /help Displays a complete list of available bot commands and their descriptions. Group administrators see all commands including advanced configuration options, while regular users see a limited list of basic commands they can use. Use this to discover what the bot can do and get quick command references. Admin Only Copy /help hashtag /start Initializes the bot and provides welcome messages. In group chats, confirms the bot is running and directs you to use /help for available commands. In private messages with the bot, shows a welcome message with an "Add to Group" button to install the bot in your Telegram group. Also handles payment confirmations when you complete a subscription purchase. Admin Only Copy /start hashtag /status Checks if the bot is operational and running properly in your group. Confirms the bot is active and provides a link to the documentation for setup instructions. Useful for verifying the bot is responsive after installation or troubleshooting connection issues. Admin Only Copy /status hashtag /get_chatid Displays your Telegram group's unique chat ID. This identifier is useful for technical configurations, integrations with other systems, or when contacting support. The chat ID is a unique number that Telegram assigns to each group and is required for certain advanced features and API integrations. pages.title: Calendar & Time | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Calendar and time management commands including /set_calendar and /get_calendar. Enables setup of recurring report generation schedules with custom start dates and cadence intervals, with all times managed in UTC timezone.

codeExamples.description: Sets up weekly report generation starting June 20, 2025 with 7-day intervals
Displays current calendar schedule configuration showing start date and cadence

useCases: Set up weekly report generation starting from a specific date
Configure automatic reports at custom intervals (7 days, 14 days, etc.)
Schedule reports starting up to 6 days in the past or any future date
Verify current calendar schedule configuration and report cadence
Manage all report timing in UTC timezone for consistency

implementationSteps.title: Set Calendar Schedule
implementationSteps.description: Use /set_calendar with start date (format: DD/MM/YYYY) and cadence (e.g., 7days) to establish recurring report generation

implementationSteps.title: Verify Configuration
implementationSteps.description: Run /get_calendar to confirm the start date and interval for automatic report generation

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Calendar & Time hashtag /set_calendar Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. For example, setting "20/06/2025 7days" will generate weekly reports starting June 20, 2025. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone. Admin Only Copy /set_calendar 20/06/2025 7days Parameters: Name Type Example start_date required 20/06/2025 cadence required 7days hashtag /get_calendar Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often reports are generated (cadence). Use this to verify your report generation schedule or to check if a calendar has been configured yet. All times are shown in UTC timezone.
