paragraphs: Sets up a recurring calendar schedule for automatic report generation. Specify a start date and how often reports should be generated (cadence in days). The bot will automatically create reports at the specified intervals starting from the chosen date. For example, setting "20/06/2025 7days" will generate weekly reports starting June 20, 2025. The start date can be up to 6 days in the past or any future date. All times are in UTC timezone.

Displays the current calendar schedule configuration for automatic reports, showing the start date and how often reports are generated (cadence). Use this to verify your report generation schedule or to check if a calendar has been configured yet. All times are shown in UTC timezone.

pages.title: Filter Setup | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive reference documentation for X Bot filter setup commands. Covers viewing, adding, removing, and clearing filters for tracking Twitter/X accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. All filter commands are admin-only and support optional named filters for organizing different tracking groups.

codeExamples.description: Add multiple Twitter/X accounts to track for monitoring posts and engagement
Add keywords and hashtags to track, using quotes for multi-word phrases
Add cashtags to a named filter group for organized token symbol tracking

useCases: Track crypto project team members and influencers separately using named filters
Monitor brand mentions and competitor activity across Twitter
Filter out spam and bot accounts from engagement reports
Organize KOL tracking by categories like team, influencers, and partners
Track multiple cryptocurrency tokens and their community discussions
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Filter Setup hashtag View Filters hashtag /list_filters Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup. Admin Only Copy /list_filters hashtag /show_filter Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking. Admin Only Copy /show_filter Copy /show_filter filter=kols Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag Add Filters hashtag /add_accounts Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors). Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @CryptoHayes filter optional team hashtag /add_keywords Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required crypto filter optional trending hashtag /add_cashtags Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $BWS filter optional tokens hashtag /add_mentions Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /add_excludes Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /add_ignore Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @SpamBot filter optional clean hashtag Remove/Clear Filters hashtag /remove_accounts Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @OldAccount filter optional kols hashtag /remove_keywords Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required oldterm filter optional trending hashtag /remove_cashtags Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example cashtags required $OLDTOKEN filter optional tokens hashtag /remove_mentions Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mentions required value filter optional influencers hashtag /remove_excludes Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example keywords required spam filter optional clean hashtag /remove_ignore Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example accounts required @ReinstatedAccount filter optional clean hashtag /clear_accounts Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional kols hashtag /clear_keywords Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional trending hashtag /clear_cashtags Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional tokens hashtag /clear_mentions Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional influencers hashtag /clear_excludes Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example filter optional clean hashtag /clear_ignore Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked. paragraphs: Lists all configured filters for the current group, showing their names and a summary of what each filter tracks. Useful for managing multiple filters and understanding your current tracking setup.

Displays the complete configuration of a filter including all tracked accounts, keywords, cashtags, mentions, exclusions, and ignored accounts. Shows the resulting X API query that will be used for tracking.

Adds one or more X (Twitter) accounts to track. Posts from these accounts will be monitored and included in reports and leaderboards. You can optionally specify a named filter to organize different account groups (e.g., team members, influencers, competitors).

Adds keywords or phrases to track in X posts. Any post containing these keywords will be monitored and included in reports. Use quotes for multi-word phrases. Keywords can include hashtags. Helps filter content by topics, brands, or specific terms relevant to your project.

Adds cashtags (token symbols) to track in X posts. Any post containing these cashtags will be monitored and included in reports. Cashtags must start with $ symbol. Useful for tracking cryptocurrency tokens, stock symbols, or project-specific ticker symbols.

Adds X handles to track when they are mentioned in posts. Any post that mentions these accounts (using @handle) will be monitored and included in reports. Different from add_accounts which tracks posts FROM accounts - this tracks posts ABOUT/MENTIONING accounts.

Adds keywords or phrases to exclude from tracking. Posts containing these excluded terms will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to remove noise, spam, or irrelevant content from your tracking.

Adds X accounts to ignore. Posts from these accounts will be filtered out and NOT included in reports, even if they match other filter criteria. Use this to exclude bot accounts, spam accounts, or specific users whose posts you don't want to track.

Removes specific X accounts from tracking. Posts from these accounts will no longer be monitored or included in reports. Use this to stop tracking accounts that are no longer relevant to your project.
Removes specific keywords or phrases from tracking. Posts containing only these keywords will no longer be monitored. Use quotes for multi-word phrases.
Removes specific cashtags from tracking. Posts containing only these cashtags will no longer be monitored.
Removes X handles from mention tracking. Posts mentioning only these accounts will no longer be monitored.
Removes keywords from the exclusion list. Posts containing these terms will no longer be automatically filtered out.
Removes X accounts from the ignore list. Posts from these accounts will now be tracked if they match filter criteria.
Removes ALL tracked accounts from the filter. Use this to reset account tracking and start fresh. This does not delete the filter itself, only clears the accounts list.
Removes ALL keywords from the filter. Use this to reset keyword tracking.
Removes ALL cashtags from the filter. Use this to reset cashtag tracking.
Removes ALL mentions from the filter. Use this to reset mention tracking.
Removes ALL excluded keywords from the filter. Posts with previously excluded terms will now be tracked.
Removes ALL ignored accounts from the filter. Posts from previously ignored accounts will now be tracked.

pages.title: Leaderboard Customization | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents X Bot leaderboard customization commands for personalizing engagement reports. Covers setting point multipliers for different engagement types (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, views), configuring HTML colors for visual reports, customizing section titles, and controlling the number of users displayed in leaderboards.

codeExamples.description: Set scoring multipliers for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and views to customize how engagement is weighted
Configure HTML hex colors for displaying different engagement metrics in visual reports
Customize the main leaderboard section title with custom text and color branding

useCases: Weight replies higher than likes to encourage meaningful conversations
Brand leaderboard reports with project-specific colors matching visual identity
Customize report titles for weekly, monthly, or campaign-specific rankings
Adjust point values to emphasize quality engagement over vanity metrics
Configure leaderboard size to highlight top 5 or top 20 performers
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Leaderboard Customization hashtag /set_points Sets the scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. These multipliers determine how many points each type of engagement contributes to a user's total score. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Copy /set_points 5 1 20 10 0.01 Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_points Retrieves and displays the current scoring multipliers for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard calculations. Shows either configured values or indicates default values are being used. Copy /get_points hashtag /set_colors Sets the HTML colors for displaying engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) in the leaderboard reports. Colors are stored as hexadecimal values and used for report generation. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes required 50 retweets required 50 replies required 50 quotes required 50 views required value hashtag /get_colors Retrieves and displays the current HTML color configuration for engagement metrics (Likes, Retweets, Replies, Quotes, Views) used in leaderboard reports. Shows either configured colors or default values if none are set. hashtag /set_top_title Sets the title text and color for the main leaderboard section in generated reports. Allows customization of the primary heading displayed at the top of leaderboard reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_best_title Sets the title text and color for the best tweet section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above the highlighted best tweet. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /set_engagement_title Sets the title text and color for the engagement scoring section in generated reports. Allows customization of the heading displayed above engagement scoring rules and metrics. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example title_text required My Title color required #FF5733 hashtag /get_titles Retrieves and displays all current title configurations for the report sections including top title (main leaderboard), best title (best tweet), and engagement title (scoring rules). Shows either configured values or default values for each section. hashtag /set_top_count Sets the number of users to display in the report leaderboard. This controls how many top-performing users are shown in the generated reports. Only group administrators can execute this command. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example count required value hashtag /get_top_count Retrieves and displays the current number of users configured to show in the report leaderboard. Shows either the configured value or indicates default value (10) is being used. pages.title: Project Configuration | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details X Bot project configuration commands for branding and customizing community identity. Covers setting project name, short and long descriptions, uploading logos, and configuring project URLs that appear in reports and on the public xbot.ninja dashboard for discovery by investors and partners.

codeExamples.description: Set the official project name that appears in all generated reports and website dashboard

codeExamples.code: /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance
codeExamples.description: Add a concise 1-2 sentence project summary for reports and dashboard visibility
Configure multiple project URLs including website, social media, and documentation links

useCases: Brand analytics reports with official project name and logo for professional presentation
Create public dashboard profile for investor discovery on xbot.ninja
Provide project context and mission statement in automated reports
Link to official website, Twitter, Discord, and GitHub from dashboard
Showcase comprehensive project information to potential partners and community
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Project Configuration hashtag /set_project_name Sets your project or community name that will appear in all generated reports, leaderboards, and on the website dashboard. This is the main identifier for your community and should be your project's official name or brand. Use this to customize reports with your project's identity instead of using generic Telegram group names. Admin Only Copy /set_project_name CryptoDAO Community Copy /set_project_name My Awesome Project Parameters: Name Type Example project_name required My Project hashtag /get_project_name Displays the current project name configured for your community. Use this to verify what name will appear in reports and on the website, or to check if a project name has been set yet. Admin Only Copy /get_project_name hashtag /set_project_description Sets a short description of your project that appears in reports and on the website. This should be a concise summary of what your project does or your community's mission (1-2 sentences). Use this to provide context about your project's goals when sharing reports with investors, partners, or community members. Admin Only Copy /set_project_description A revolutionary DeFi platform for community governance Parameters: Name Type Example description required value hashtag /get_project_description Displays the current project description configured for your community. Use this to review the short description that appears in reports, or to check if a description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_long_description Sets a detailed, comprehensive description of your project for the website dashboard. Unlike the short description, this can be multiple paragraphs and include more in-depth information about your project's mission, features, roadmap, and team. This appears on your public dashboard page to provide visitors with complete project details. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example long_description required value hashtag /get_project_long_description Displays the current detailed project description configured for the website dashboard. Use this to review the comprehensive description that visitors see on your public dashboard page, or to check if a long description has been set. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_logo Uploads your project logo that will appear on reports and the website dashboard. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. Send your logo as a photo or file (PNG, JPG, or GIF recommended). The logo should be square or rectangular and will be displayed prominently in all generated reports and on your public dashboard page. Admin Only hashtag /get_project_logo Displays the URL of your currently configured project logo. Use this to verify which logo will appear in reports and on the website, or to share the logo URL with team members. If no logo is set, you'll receive a message prompting you to upload one with /set_project_logo. Admin Only hashtag /set_project_urls Sets important links for your project including website, social media, documentation, and other resources. These URLs appear on the website dashboard as clickable links, making it easy for visitors to connect with your project across different platforms. You can include your website, Twitter/X, Discord, Telegram, GitHub, documentation, or any other relevant links. Separate multiple URLs with spaces or commas. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example urls required https://myproject.com hashtag /get_project_urls Displays all project URLs currently configured for your community. Use this to review which links appear on the website dashboard, verify URLs are correct, or check if any URLs have been set. pages.title: Raid Commands | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive guide to X Bot raid commands for coordinating community engagement campaigns on Twitter/X posts. Covers launching raids with customizable targets, tracking real-time progress, managing active raids, reviewing history, configuring defaults, and setting up automatic raids for top-performing content.

codeExamples.description: Launch a coordinated raid on a Twitter post with specific engagement targets for likes and retweets
Configure default raid settings including engagement targets and duration for future raids
Enable automatic raids that trigger for the best tweet of the day with specified parameters

useCases: Amplify important project announcements by coordinating community engagement
Support partner projects with organized community raid campaigns
Drive visibility for product launches and major updates
Automatically boost top-performing community content daily
Track raid performance metrics to measure community participation
Celebrate when engagement targets are met to motivate community
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Overview X Bot Installation X Bot Commands chevron-right Filter Setup Basic Commands Advanced Filtering Project Configuration Raid Commands Schedule Management Calendar & Time Reports Leaderboard Customization Topic Management Admin Subscription & Credits Getting Support gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Marketplace Solutions chevron-right X Bot chevron-right X Bot Commands Raid Commands hashtag /raidx Launches a coordinated community raid on an X (Twitter) post to boost engagement. Community members receive a raid announcement with the target post and engagement goals (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks). The bot tracks progress in real-time and celebrates when targets are met. Raids help amplify important posts, support partners, or drive visibility for project announcements. Admin Only Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes=50 retweets=20 Copy /raidx https://x.com/username/status/123456 duration=1h mute=yes Parameters: Name Type Example post_url required https://x.com/username/status/123456 likes optional 50 retweets optional 20 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 duration optional 1h mute optional yes hashtag /raid_status Displays real-time progress of active raids, showing current engagement numbers vs. targets for likes, retweets, replies, quotes, and bookmarks. You can check a specific raid by ID or see the latest active raid. Use this to monitor how your community is progressing toward raid goals and motivate participation. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id optional value hashtag /raid_stop Cancels an active raid immediately, stopping progress tracking and notifications. Use this when you need to end a raid early - for example, if the target post was deleted, targets were already met naturally, or the raid was started by mistake. The raid status will be marked as "CANCELLED" in the raid history. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example raid_id required value hashtag /raid_history Displays a list of previous raids with their completion status, targets, and results. Review past raid performance to see which raids were successful, how long they took, and engagement levels achieved. Useful for analyzing community participation patterns and planning future raids. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example limit optional value hashtag /set_raid_defaults Configures default settings for all future raids including target engagement numbers, duration, auto-mute behavior, and whether raids are enabled. These defaults are used when you start a raid without specifying custom parameters. Set these once to match your community's typical raid goals, then override them per-raid when needed with /raidx parameters. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example likes optional 30 retweets optional 15 replies optional 10 quotes optional 5 bookmarks optional 20 duration optional 1h mute optional no enabled optional yes hashtag /get_raid_defaults Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_message_behavior Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example mode required value hashtag /get_raid_message_behavior Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat. Admin Only hashtag /set_raid_image Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message. Admin Only hashtag /auto_raid_config Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior. Admin Only Parameters: Name Type Example enabled optional no duration optional 360 delay optional 5 likes optional 100 retweets optional 50 replies optional 50 quotes optional 50 bookmarks optional 50 mute optional yes hashtag /get_auto_raid_config Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config. Displays the current default raid settings including target numbers for all engagement types, default duration, auto-mute behavior, and raid enable/disable status. Use this to review your configured defaults before starting a raid or to verify settings after using /set_raid_defaults.

Controls how the bot updates raid progress messages in the chat. Choose between three modes: "edit" (updates the existing message cleanly), "repost" (deletes and resends to keep it at the bottom of the chat), or "pin" (updates and pins the message to keep it visible at the top). Different modes suit different community preferences and chat activity levels.
Shows the currently configured raid message update mode (edit, repost, or pin) and explains what that mode does. Use this to verify how raid progress messages will be displayed in your chat.

Uploads a custom image that appears in all raid announcement messages for your community. After running this command, the bot will prompt you to send an image file. This branded image helps make raid announcements more visually appealing and recognizable to your community. The image is displayed prominently in every raid message.

Configures automatic raids that trigger for the "Best Tweet of the Day" selected during daily report generation. When enabled, the bot automatically starts a raid on the top-performing post a few minutes after the report is published. This helps consistently boost your best content without manual raid creation. Configure raid duration, delay before starting, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute behavior.

Displays the current auto-raid configuration including whether it's enabled, the raid duration, start delay after report, target engagement numbers, and auto-mute setting. Use this to review your auto-raid setup or verify changes after using /auto_raid_config.

implementationSteps.title: Add Bot to Telegram Group

It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups. X analytics you can trust X Bot uses the official X API to ensure accuracy and reliability. We tested several cheaper alternatives and even built our own "posts crawler," but none matched the accuracy of the official API. Many third-party analytics tools you find online show up to 50% discrepancies in their metrics. With X Bot, you get data directly from the source you can trust. hashtag The Problem X Bot Solves In the crypto space, it's challenging to: Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics. hashtag Key Features hashtag Analytics & Tracking Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions) Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency) View leaderboards ranking top performers by engagement hashtag Performance Scoring X Bot uses a scoring algorithm that weighs different engagement types based on their value. The weights are fully configurable to match each project's specific needs and priorities. paragraphs: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides trusted X (Twitter) analytics for crypto communities. It enables tracking, analysis, and reporting of X activity directly within Telegram groups.
In the crypto space, it's challenging to:
Get a trusted measure of community engagement and the real impact of hired KOLs' profitability
Identify projects with genuine community traction vs. artificial engagement
Find and hire KOL teams that deliver actual results
Track and measure authentic X (Twitter) engagement
Verify influencer performance with transparent metrics
Discover legitimate investment opportunities backed by real community activity
X Bot solves these problems by providing verifiable X analytics that distinguish authentic engagement from vanity metrics.
Track X (Twitter) accounts, hashtags, cashtags, and mentions
Monitor engagement metrics (likes, retweets, replies, quotes, bookmarks, impressions)
Generate automated scheduled reports with performance insights (up to 12-hour frequency)

Quotes (×3): High-value engagement - adds commentary while sharing
Bookmarks (×2): Content worth saving for later reference
Impressions (×0.001): Reach metric - how many people saw the post

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains BWS platform fee structure and pricing model. Covers how blockchain network fees are managed, testnet vs mainnet pricing, and the $BWS token usage. Provides pricing examples for creating NFTs using BWS services.
useCases: Calculate costs for creating 100 NFTs on Polygon blockchain
Test blockchain solutions for free using Mumbai or Sepolia networks
Monitor monthly blockchain credit usage and billing
Convert USD to network tokens for mainnet transactions
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right Fees Calculator arrow-up-right-from-square API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Platform Fees We take care of everything. Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”. circle-info Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized. Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience. hashtag Fees Calculator Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience. circle-check In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use. If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks. You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free. We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged. Using test networks does not consume $BWS tokens. Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details, You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free. When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit. Every solution requires $BWS tokens to execute and fund the developer's community and platform maintenance. Please read the platform White Paper arrow-up-right to know more about the $BWS token and its tokenomics. hashtag Pricing Example Suppose you subscribe to Blockchain Web Services and want to create 100 new NFTs on the Polygon blockchain using BWS.NFT.zK arrow-up-right to create the NFTs, and BWS.IPFS.Upload arrow-up-right to save your images to IPFS. Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you: Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan. hashtag How do I pay those Fees? To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage. circle-info BLOCKCHAIN CREDIT FEE We use the Blockchain Credit Fee as the minimum billable unit, 0.01 USD. If, for example, you spend 2 USD creating NFTs, we will add 200 blockchain credit fee units to your next bill. Credits Usage balance. Previous Quick Start chevron-left Next API How Tos chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Fees Calculator Pricing Example How do I pay those Fees? Was this helpful? sun-bright desktop moon Copy 100 IPFS Images, included = 0 USD 100 NFTs x 0.02 Network Fee = 2 USD sun-bright desktop moon

paragraphs: We take care of everything.
Calling blockchain smart contracts requires funds: “It is the fuel that allows it to operate”.

Fees compensate miners for their computational efforts in processing and securing transactions on the network. They are fundamental to the blockchain's sustainability, security, and efficiency, ensuring the network remains robust and decentralized.
Blockchain Web Services (BWS) aims to transform the blockchain market by making innovative solutions accessible to a broad audience, including those without technical or Web3 experience.
Interacting with blockchain requires paying fees, so it's important to understand how Blockchain Web Services manages those fees to offer our customers the best user experience.

In any scenario, we handle all the blockchain complexities. You interact with the blockchain(s) we support using our simplified API, and you don't need to worry about anything other than naming the blockchain you want to use.
If you haven't entered your credit card, you can test our blockchain solutions for free using the Mumbai or Sepolia test networks.
You can run 100 blockchain transactions for free.
We use our token pools to pay network fees, and you are not charged.
Once you move into subscription mode by entering your credit card details,
You can run 1000 testnet transactions for free.

When running on main networks, we do real-time trading, convert US dollars to the required network token, and pay the fees for you. You will then be charged for those fees on your next bill, using 0.01 USD as the minimum billable unit.
Creating those 100 NFTs will cost you:
Your next bill will show an extra 2 USD to your monthly plan.
To check your current monthly cost, go to the Billing menu and check your credit usage.

JavaScript/jQuery code to insert data into blockchain and handle the response

implementationSteps.title: Get API Key
implementationSteps.description: Authenticate your API requests by obtaining and using your BWS API key. All requests without an API key will return errors.

implementationSteps.title: Write Data to Blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Make a POST request to /v1/smart-contracts/call with your solution parameters (BWS.Blockchain.Hash, network, operation, and data). Receive a jobId in the response.

implementationSteps.title: Check Job Status
implementationSteps.description: Use the jobId to fetch results via /v1/smart-contracts/fetch. Wait for completion to receive blockchain receipt and Certificate of Trust.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down Quick Start Writing "Hello World!" to the blockchain in just one call! hashtag Get your API key Your API requests should be authenticated using your API key (any request that doesn't include an API key will return an error). circle-info Learn how to Get your API Key . hashtag Your First BWS API Request We're going to use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash arrow-up-right solution to save a message to the blockchain. hashtag Write "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/call Let's write "Hello World!" to the Matchain blockchain network without managing network fees or Web3 wallets by calling a regular Web2 API. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.Blockchain.Hash version * number 1 network * string matchain operation * string insert parameters * JSON { key: "mykey", value: "Hello World!" } Please copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your API Key). curl Javascript Phyton If executed correctly, check for jobId in the response body part. We will use it to verify that the blockchain operation has been completed correctly and to receive the blockchain transaction receipt and Certificate of Trust . API Call response hashtag Check for Status To check for job completion and results, use the jobId you got from the previous call. circle-info Please note that interacting with blockchain(s) is asynchronous, so it may take some time for the operation to finish and for the blockchain receipt to be received. hashtag Fetch for the blockchain job status and results. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/smart-contracts/fetch Using the previous call response jobId we fetch the blockchain job started previously. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description jobId * String (use previously returned jobId, as for example 59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e) Copy & Paste one of the following code snippets and run it (remember to use your own API Key). curl Javascript Phyton You should receive a response indicating the completed job and the related blockchain receipt and certificate. Fetch API Call Response Congratulations! In just one call, you used BWS to save data into the Web3 blockchain ecosystem. pages.summary: Solution overview explaining BWS.Blockchain.Hash as a blockchain-based hash database. Describes hash database concepts, use cases for client data management, and available networks including Matchain with smart contract addresses.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash Solution Overview Use the blockchain to efficiently store data in blockchain(s). BWS.Blockchain.Hash simplifies saving data to the blockchain, enabling you to use it as a hash database while abstracting the complexities of blockchain infrastructure. hashtag What is a Hash Database? A hash database is a type of database that uses hash keys to efficiently retrieve and store data. Each record is assigned a unique key, often derived from the data or predefined attributes, which acts as an identifier for quick lookups. Hash databases are known for their speed and scalability, making them ideal for applications requiring rapid data access. hashtag An example: saving client data to the blockchain. Businesses can use BWS.Blockchain.Hash to manage client data by storing essential information (e.g., customer profiles or transaction records) in a blockchain-based hash database. The system uses unique keys, such as client IDs or transaction numbers, to retrieve data effortlessly. Here's how this could work: Client Information Use a unique client ID as the hash key to link to stored client details or references. Data Retrieval Quickly access data associated with a hash key for reporting, auditing, or verification purposes. Enhanced Trust Leverage blockchain’s immutable nature to ensure the integrity and transparency of stored records. hashtag Why Choose BWS.Blockchain.Hash? This solution is perfect for individuals and businesses that want to take advantage of blockchain as a trusted and efficient hash-based storage system. With BWS.Blockchain.Hash , you can store and retrieve data securely, gain the benefits of blockchain transparency, and provide clients with a Certificate of Trust for each entry—ensuring their data is handled responsibly and verifiably. hashtag Available Networks circle-exclamation We're currently updating this solution smart contracts for Polygon (main) and Mumbai (test). Please use Matchain. implementationSteps.title: Prepare File Content

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Operations Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. hashtag Upload to IPFS Use this operation to upload content to IPFS and get the IPFS Content Identifier (CID). POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the IPFS CID and useful URIs. Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "info" : { "cid" : "QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "uris" : { "ipfs" : "ipfs://QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" , "url" : "https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmXmCX9S6ANVjYJh3rJmXjqgYtYv7WZLUDL2XCwdPrvUwN" } } } hashtag Upload to IPFS Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption description string Short description of your content (will be returned when listing your IPFS files ). content JSON / base64 Content to save on the IPFS network. We currently support: JSON (just pass the JSON or the stringified string) File sent as a base64 encoded string circle-exclamation Please note that when uploading your file: You must optimize your content to be less than 1 MB in size (there is a hard limit of 5MB when uploading files to IPFS using our API). We currently support JSON, images, and PDF files. If your use case requires higher file sizes or other file types, please contact us. hashtag Upload to IPFS Examples Upload an Image using nodejs Upload JSON using cURL hashtag Upload to IPFS call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the IPFS Content Identifier (CID) along with the corresponding IPFS URI and URL. These details facilitate easy access and reference to the uploaded content on the IPFS network. hashtag List IPFS Files Get the list of your IPFS files POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.IPFS.Upload operation * string list parameters JSON check Method Parameters 200 The list of files you uploaded to IPFS hashtag List IPFS Files Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption from long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files from. to long Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds representing a timestamp to select IPFS files to. hashtag LIST IPFS Files Request Example cURL hashtag List IPFS Files call Response Once executed correctly, you will get a list of the IPFS uploaded files using BWS API. That list will contain for each file: the timestamp of creation ( Unix time arrow-up-right in milliseconds), the IPFS Content Identifier (CID), the file type, and the URIs to easily access the file IPFS URI, web URL - using the BWS IPFS Gateway to fetch the file. pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS IPFS API Service enables easy uploading of images, PDF, and JSON files to the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS). The service provides decentralized, permanent storage with content addressing through unique CIDs, accessible via the ipfs.bws.ninja gateway for seamless integration with web applications.
useCases: Store NFT metadata and asset images for blockchain applications
Host static resources like logos and graphics for web projects
Share structured JSON data in a transparent and verifiable way
Implement decentralized content delivery systems
Ensure data integrity and provenance for digital assets
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right Solution Overview Operations BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.IPFS.Upload Solution Overview Easily upload Images, PDF and JSON files to IPFS. BWS IPFS API Service leverages the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) , a distributed, peer-to-peer storage protocol designed to create a permanent and decentralized method of storing and sharing data. IPFS breaks files into smaller chunks, distributes them across the network, and assigns each file a unique content identifier (CID) for efficient retrieval. hashtag BWS IPFS Gateway Use ipfs.bws.ninja as the gateway to access the files you have uploaded to IPFS using BWS. circle-info An IPFS gateway acts as a bridge between the decentralized IPFS network and traditional web browsers, allowing users to access content stored on IPFS using standard HTTP protocols. This makes IPFS content easily accessible without requiring specialized software or configuration. Check out our lovely unicorn on IPFS using the BWS Gateway: https://ipfs.bws.ninja/ipfs/QmU7avmnTb4iVbNGCHMJmdEbsx9nRtUx7dWGpKeE7zjo8T arrow-up-right hashtag Key Features of IPFS Decentralized Storage Files are stored across multiple nodes, enhancing reliability and fault tolerance. Content Addressing Data is retrieved using its CID, ensuring that files are immutable and tamper-proof. Global Accessibility IPFS data can be accessed by any node in the network, fostering collaboration and resilience. hashtag Supported File Types Our service supports uploading images. PDF and JSON files , making it ideal for use cases where these formats are predominant. Examples include: NFT Metadata and Assets Storing JSON metadata files and associated images for blockchain applications. Decentralized Content Delivery Hosting static resources like logos or graphics for web or app projects. Data Integrity and Provenance Sharing structured data (e.g., JSON) in a transparent, verifiable way. pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page explains NFT attributes (traits) that define unique characteristics of non-fungible tokens. It covers the metadata structure including trait_type and value pairs, demonstrating how traits like fur color, eye type, and accessories differentiate NFTs and influence their perceived value and rarity.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure showing how traits are defined with trait_type and value pairs for a digital cat collection

useCases: Create unique digital art collections with distinct characteristics
Define rarity levels for NFT collectibles
Build trait-based filtering systems for NFT marketplaces
Implement attribute-driven NFT valuation systems
Design gaming assets with specific character properties
pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK NFT Attributes (traits) Making your NFT unique. Non-fungible tokens (NFTs) represent unique digital assets on blockchain platforms. Those assets can define associated traits , which are specific attributes or characteristics that detail each NFT. In a practical scenario, an NFT's metadata might look something like this for a digital art collection of animated cats: Copy { "name" : "Animated Cat #001" , "description" : "A unique animated cat with distinct features." , "image" : "ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowkiu" , "attributes" : [ { "trait_type" : "Fur Color" , "value" : "Black" } , { "trait_type" : "Eye Type" , "value" : "Round" } , { "trait_type" : "Accessories" , "value" : "Hat" } ] } Within the metadata, the attributes array contains the various traits of the NFT. Each trait is defined by a trait_type , which specifies the category of the trait (e.g., "Fur Color" or "Rarity"), and a value , which indicates the specific characteristic of that trait (e.g., "Black" or "Ultra-Rare"). Traits encapsulated within this metadata not only bring out the uniqueness of each token but can also influence its perceived value, especially if some traits are considered rarer than others. BWS.NFT.zK allows you to create an NFT by passing those traits . circle-exclamation Please note that it's the usage of those traits when visualizing your NFT that you must take care of and validate. For example, if you use the provided Certificate of Trust for your users' experience use case, you must check they are correctly displayed. Previous Operations chevron-left Next Blockchain Badges chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful? sun-bright desktop moon sun-bright desktop moon

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: Submit POST request to /v1/call with solution 'BWS.NFT.zK', operation 'new', and parameters including name, description, image URL, and optional attributes. Receive jobId for tracking.

implementationSteps.title: Check Transaction Status
implementationSteps.description: Use fetch API with the returned jobId to check blockchain transaction status and retrieve NFT details including transaction hash, IPFS hashes, and transfer codes.

implementationSteps.title: Transfer Ownership
implementationSteps.description: Use 'transfer' operation with nftId and recipient wallet address, or 'send' operation with email address for users without Web3 wallets. Track transfer completion via jobId.

Check the blockchain transaction status and receipt using the fetch API and the provided jobId. hashtag Creates a new NFT. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string new parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id Copy { "statusCode" : 200 , "statusMessage" : "" , "info" : { "jobId" : "59d0f46b-0b58-4972-8aca-51d06f69f25e" } } hashtag Create NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption name string Name of your NFT. description string The NFT description. image URL (or IPFS URI) This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image. attributes optional JSON These are the attributes of your NFT. hashtag Create NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Create NFT Fetch Response chevron-right Create NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag The fetch operation , once the NFT creation job is completed, will return the following: Parameter Type Description guid string NFT unique id network string Network the NFT has been created in. nftTxHash string Blockchain transaction hash nftId string NFT id nftIPFSHash string NFT metadata file IPFS hash nftImageIPFSHash string NFT image file IPFS hash nftJson json NFT metadata json url json (check NFT Urls ) status string The NFT status: "created" or "transferred" transferCodes json (check Transfer Codes ) hashtag NFT Urls hashtag Easy-to-use URLs for you to get access to NFT's most relevant data. Parameter Type Description nft URL NFT metada URL image URL NFT image URL transaction URL NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL hashtag Transfer Codes hashtag Transfer codes that are required to transfer ownership when sending an NFT through email (check NFT Ownership to learn more). Parameter Type Description owner string Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email. receiver string The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer. hashtag List NFTs hashtag Lists your NFTs. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Get the list of your NFTs. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK operation * string list parameters JSON check Methods Parameters 200 Returns the list of your NFTs. hashtag List NFT Method Parameters circle-info If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned. Parameter Type Desciption from long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from. to long Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to. hashtag List IPFS Files call Response hashtag LIST NFT Request Example cURL hashtag List NFT Response The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in. chevron-right List NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Transfer NFT circle-info This operation is asynchronous. (once executed, check the results using fetch API and the provided jobId) hashtag Transfer an NFT ownership. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK version * number 1 network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string transfer parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Transfer NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. address string Wallet address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Transfer NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Transfer NFT Fetch Response The fetch operation for the NFT transfer API call will mainly state if the transfer has been successful ( completed ) and the related costs. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag hashtag Send NFT by Email hashtag Send an NFT by email. POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address. hashtag Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.zK network * string check available networks arrow-up-right operation * string send parameters * JSON check Method Parameters 200 Returns the related job Id hashtag Send NFT Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption nftId string The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred. email string Email address to send the NFT to (check NFT Owerneship ) hashtag Send NFT Request Example cURL hashtag Send NFT by Email Response As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer. chevron-right Transfer NFT Fetch Response Example hashtag Previous Solution Overview chevron-left Next NFT Attributes (traits) chevron-right Last updated 1 month ago Was this helpful? Transfer NFT Method Parameters Transfer NFT Fetch Response Send NFT by Email Send an NFT by email. paragraphs: BWS.NFT.zK API Operations.
Use this operation to create (Mint) a new NFT on a selected blockchain Network.
This is the URL or IPFS URI of your NFT image.
These are the attributes of your NFT.
Network the NFT has been created in.
Blockchain transaction hash
NFT metadata file IPFS hash
NFT image file IPFS hash
The NFT status: "created" or "transferred"
NFT blockchain transaction explorer URL
Owner code is required to execute a transfer when sending an NFT over email.
The receiver code the new owner should indicate to execute the NFT transfer.
Get the list of your NFTs.
If no range is defined, the last 20 NFTs are returned.
Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs from.
Number of milliseconds representing a timestamp to select NFTs to.
The list operation returns the list of your NFTs, including NFT-relevant data and the network you created the NFT in.
Transfer an NFT ownership by using the new owner's wallet or email address.
The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred.
Send an NFT by using the new owner's email address.
The unique identifier of the NFT to be transferred.
As a confirmation message response, if the NFT send is confirmed you will get your NFT-related details, including the codes that the new owner will have to use to execute a blockchain NFT transfer.

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Comprehensive guide to NFT ownership management in BWS.NFT.zK, covering custody models, transfer methods, and data storage. Explains transferring ownership via wallet addresses or email, IPFS-based metadata and image hosting, and supported blockchain networks including Matchain, Polygon, and Ethereum with verified smart contracts.

codeExamples.description: Example JSON structure for creating NFT using a web-addressable URL for the image, which will be automatically uploaded to IPFS
Example NFT metadata using IPFS URI format for pre-uploaded images, demonstrating how metadata is structured and stored on IPFS

useCases: Transfer NFT ownership to users without Web3 wallets using email addresses
Custody NFTs within BWS account before transferring to final owners
Host NFT images and metadata on IPFS for permanent decentralized storage
Deploy NFTs across multiple blockchain networks (Matchain, Polygon, Ethereum)
Enable recipients to claim NFT ownership and transfer to their own wallets

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT with Initial Custody
implementationSteps.description: Create NFT using BWS.NFT.zK API with image URL or IPFS URI. The NFT is initially linked to your BWS account with custody until transferred.

implementationSteps.title: Choose Transfer Method
implementationSteps.description: Select transfer method: use Transfer NFT operation with blockchain wallet address, or use email address for users without Web3 wallets.

implementationSteps.title: Complete Ownership Transfer
implementationSteps.description: For email transfers, recipient receives notification with transfer codes. They can claim ownership and optionally transfer to their wallet address using the provided interface.

pages.fullContent: bars BWS API Docs search circle-xmark Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More ellipsis chevron-down Welcome! chevron-right Quick Start Platform Fees chevron-right API How Tos chevron-right Certificate Of Trust Media Assets chevron-right PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Save chevron-right BWS.Blockchain.Hash chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK chevron-right Solution Overview chevron-right NFT Ownership arrow-up-right-from-square NFT Data Location arrow-up-right-from-square Available Networks arrow-up-right-from-square Operations chevron-right NFT Attributes (traits) Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges chevron-right FAN Game Cube chevron-right ESG Credits chevron-right X Bot chevron-right gitbook Powered by GitBook block-quote On this page chevron-down copy Copy chevron-down PLATFORM APIs chevron-right BWS.NFT.zK Solution Overview Easily create NFTs. hashtag NFT Ownership When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options: Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address. Transfer ownership to an email address. circle-info Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account. hashtag Transfer NFT Ownership to a Wallet If you want to transfer an NFT ownership to another wallet address, you can simply use the Transfer NFT operation indicating the new blockchain wallet address owner. hashtag Transfer NFT Ownership to an Email Address If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address. Email received for the new NFT owner to hold and get ownership We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to. Transfer ownership page for wasy NFT transfers circle-info Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address. hashtag NFT Metadata and Image Location hashtag NFT Image Your NFT image is always hosted in IPFS. To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as: a web addressable URL, or, as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki). circle-info API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image. paragraphs: When you create a new NFT using BWS.NFT.zK (Zero Knowledge) solution, the NFT will initially be linked to your BWS account. In order to transfer an NFT ownership, you have two options:
Transfer ownership to a blockchain wallet address.
Transfer ownership to an email address.
Before you transfer your NFT, we will do the custody, and ownership is mapped with your BWS Account.
If you want to transfer an NFT ownership, but the new owner does not have a Web3 Wallet (or you don't know it), you can simply transfer the ownership by using his email address.
We will send the new owner an email notification indicating he's the owner of the NFT. If he wants to transfer the ownership to his own Wallet, he will be able to.
Please note that once you transfer the ownership using an email address, ONLY the email address owner will be able to transfer the NFT to a new Wallet address.
To create an NFT using BWS.NFT.zK, you pass the NFT image as:
a web addressable URL, or,
as an IPFS URI (for example, ipfs://QmcduEBAppXxnyn37deHHf33Ep7cPbYxn1mH36Nvvowki).
API Call JSON Example using a URL for the NFT image.
In all the scenarios, your NFT metadata will point to an image stored in IPFS, as we will upload your image to IPFS if required.
When a new NFT is created, we create and upload the NFT metadata to IPFS,
